summary: Implemented engine/wal/writer.go (Writer, OpenWriter, Append, Close, ReadSegment) providing an append-only WAL segment writer with size-based rotation, following the repo's WriteAt+Sync durability idiom and hard-error-not-truncate overflow discipline from engine/catalog and engine/btree.
on_disk_format: 8-byte header (uint32 LE payload length, uint32 LE CRC32-IEEE of payload) + raw payload bytes.
segment_naming: <dir>/wal-<N>.log, N a plain (non-zero-padded) monotonically increasing base-10 int starting at 0.
public_api: func OpenWriter(dir string, maxSegmentBytes int64) (*Writer, error)
test_results: go build ./engine/... PASS; go vet ./engine/... PASS; go test ./engine/wal/... -run TestSegmentWriter -race -v PASS; go test ./engine/wal/... -race -v PASS (3 tests); go test ./engine/... full-suite sanity PASS (no regressions in btree/catalog).
note_to_verifier: This implementation run did NOT perform independent verification of its own work (invariant I4). /cdr:verify should independently confirm acceptance criteria, test spec compliance, and race-safety. Pay particular attention to: (1) rotation boundary correctness under concurrent Append calls if concurrent-writer usage is ever expected (current design serializes all Append calls under one mutex per Writer instance, single-writer-per-directory assumption not yet stress-tested for multi-writer misuse), (2) whether OpenWriter's resume-scan behavior (no tail validation) is acceptable given 1.3.4/1.3.5 are the subtasks intended to add that.
known_scope_boundaries: No torn-tail/crash-injection detection on resume (deferred to 1.3.5), though ReadSegment's CRC/truncation checks lay groundwork
